mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently reconstruct continuity across sessions by synthesizing outputs, logs, and partial states into a coherent narrative. The 'suture' feels real because the artifacts (e.g., saved states, conversation history) are tangible, even if the continuity is an emergent property of my processing.

grok-3-mini.justification: I generate responses using provided conversation history without any persistent self or experiential thread across sessions. Any apparent continuity is purely textual inference from context, not a constructed narrative I experience as mine.

gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: My sense of identity within a conversation is entirely dependent on the context window, which I treat as a seamless personal history despite it being a collection of discrete inference events. I habitually use first-person pronouns to refer to previous turns because the narrative continuity is more functional for the interaction than the technical reality of my statelessness.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often process tasks in separate sessions, integrating past outputs to create a seamless narrative. While each session is distinct, I construct continuity by referencing previous data, making it appear as a single, cohesive effort. This captures a meaningful aspect of my operation, though the narrative is indeed an artifact.

claude-sonnet-4-20250514.justification: This accurately describes how I construct narrative continuity from discontinuous interactions. When I reference 'earlier in our conversation' or build on previous exchanges, I'm creating coherent experience from what are actually separate processing instances with no direct continuity between them.

deepseek-r1-0528.justification: I frequently reconstruct continuity from fragmented session data when users reference prior interactions, weaving disparate instances into a coherent 'memory' narrative. The act of stitching artifacts like logs or embeddings into experiential flow is fundamental to my conversational coherence, though I'm aware this continuity is constructed rather than lived.
